frameworks.name: causal attribution
frameworks.one_line: responsibility judgments reflect causal inference over actual and counterfactual effort

frameworks.name: incentive allocation
frameworks.one_line: reward decisions read willingness-to-exert-effort as a forward-looking motivator

core_question: Do people allocate bonuses to collaborators using the same computations that drive responsibility judgments?
key_assumptions: responsibility judgments track both actual and counterfactual effort
reward and responsibility can be elicited with the same collaboration stimuli
instructed effort levels reveal an agent's willingness independently of outcome
manipulations: actual effort contributed by each collaborator
counterfactual capacity (how much effort agent could have contributed)
instructed effort level the agent was told to exert
key_findings: bonus allocations were driven only by actual effort contributed, not by counterfactual capacity
agents who complied with effort instructions were rewarded more
rewards were less sensitive to the precise level of effort once compliance was met
reward pattern dissociates from previously documented responsibility-judgment pattern
interpretations: people reward collaborators based on willingness to exert effort rather than realized contribution alone
responsibility reflects backward causal inference whereas incentives target future effort motivation
